candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, it is known that $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$, and the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D. If $AC=6\,cm$, what is the length of $AD$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect $BD$,\\
since the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D,\\
it follows that $AD=BD$,\\
hence $\angle A=\angle ABD$,\\
since $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$,\\
it follows $\angle A=\angle C=\frac{1}{2}\left(180^\circ-120^\circ\right)=30^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle ABD=30^\circ$,\\
hence $\angle CBD=90^\circ$,\\
thus $CD=2BD$,\\
hence $CD=2AD$,\\
thus $AC=AD+CD=AD+2AD=3AD$,\\
further, since $AC=6\text{cm}$,\\
it follows $AD=\boxed{2\text{cm}}$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。
